title: Corn Capsicum Chaat
description: Sweet corn and capsicum tossed with onion, tomato, lemon, and chaat masala—a produce-led snack or light lunch.
explanation: This chaat uses vegetables as the bulk instead of fried sev or puri. Corn brings sweetness and energy; capsicum adds crunch and color.
Standard version includes onion, so it is not Jain or no-onion/no-garlic. Nut-free, soy-free, and gluten-free as written.
ingredients: 1.5 cups Boiled or steamed Sweet Corn kernels
Optional: 1 boiled Potato, diced, for a fuller bowl
instructions: Boil or steam corn until tender; cool slightly. Dice capsicum, onion, and tomato.
Combine corn, capsicum, onion, tomato, and optional chilli and potato in a bowl.
Add lemon, chaat masala, roasted cumin, and salt. Toss well.
Finish with coriander. Serve immediately as a snack or light lunch.
stepNotes: Use fresh or frozen corn; drain well so the chaat is not watery.
Raw capsicum should be finely diced for easier eating.
Chaat masala varies in saltiness—taste before adding more salt.
Optional potato makes it more filling for lunch.
Best eaten fresh; capsicum softens if it sits in lemon too long.

variationSections.title: Produce upgrades
items: Add cucumber or pomegranate for more freshness.
Use grilled corn for a smoky edge.
Mix in boiled chickpeas when you want more protein.

variationSections.title: Onion-free note
items: Skip onion and add extra capsicum, tomato, and coriander for a no-onion variation (not the default).
A little hing in the lemon mix can add savoriness.
